title: Namespaces, Metadata, Labels, Selectors, Ownership, and Deletion · API Object Lens: K8s Label Selector Contracts
summary: Several teams share a cluster where a selector overlaps another release, an owner is recreated, and a finalizer leaves a namespace terminating. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s label selector contracts increment in resource identity and lifecycle policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design stable recommended labels and equality or set selectors without accidental overlap, mutable identity, or controller mismatch.
Distinguish namespaced and cluster-scoped resources, default namespaces, cross-namespace references, quotas, and isolation limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Label Selector Contracts · Theory K8s Label Selector Contracts
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory namespace contains similarly named objects, copied labels, a dangling owner reference, and a finalizer blocking deletion. Design names, labels, selectors, annotations, ownership, garbage collection, quota boundaries, and deletion evidence. Competency probe: Design stable recommended labels and equality or set selectors without accidental overlap, mutable identity, or controller mismatch.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name context, namespace, API identity, desired spec, observed status, controller authority, workload, network, storage, identity, policy, and failure-domain boundaries; predict reconciliation; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example namespace contains similarly named objects, copied labels, a dangling owner reference, and a finalizer blocking deletion. Design names, labels, selectors, annotations, ownership, garbage collection, quota boundaries, and deletion evidence. Competency probe: Design stable recommended labels and equality or set selectors without accidental overlap, mutable identity, or controller mismatch. A defensible solution applies K8s Label Selector Contracts, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to resource identity and lifecycle policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-metadata-namespaces-ownership-3
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-metadata-namespaces-ownership-3
    artifact: metadata-policy.yaml
    decision: worked k8s metadata namespaces ownership 3
    environment: shared multi-tenant cluster under Restricted Pod Security
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      namespace: intake
      labels: app.kubernetes.io/name=status-api
      selector-owner: deployment-status-api
      owner-uid: retained
      deletion: foreground
      finalizer-controller: archive-cleanup
    verify: kubectl get all -n intake --show-labels -o json && kubectl get namespace intake -o json
    failure: Reject namespace-as-sandbox claims, overlapping selectors, name-only owner matching, or blind finalizer removal.
    repair: Separate scope and security, stabilize identity labels, trace owner UIDs and dependents, and identify external cleanup before deletion.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Changing a controller selector is a harmless way to retarget an existing workload. This fails the K8s Label Selector Contracts contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
